books.title: Darby O’Gill and the Good People
books.author: Herminie Templeton Kavanagh
books.summary: When Tipperary man Darby O’Gill is imprisoned by the fairies of Sleive-na-mon in their home under the hollow mountain, he starts a lasting friendship with their King Brian Connors.

books.title: The Return of Tarzan
books.author: Edgar Rice Burroughs
books.summary: Tarzan attempts to leave behind life in the jungle but rapidly discovers that life among humans contains just as many dangers.

books.title: The Autobiography of Benjamin Franklin
books.author: Benjamin Franklin
books.summary: Benjamin Franklin writes about his youth, adventures, and achievements.

books.title: The Lives and Opinions of Eminent Philosophers
books.author: Diogenes Laërtius
books.summary: Brief biographies of the prominent philosophers of ancient Greece.

books.title: The Princess and the Goblin
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A princess and her young friend valiantly fend off a goblin attack with help from a ghostly relative.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Ring Lardner
books.summary: A collection of short stories by Ring Lardner.

books.title: Festus
books.author: Philip James Bailey
books.summary: A man goes on a quest for knowledge, truth, and understanding in the face of the complexities of existence and spirituality.

books.title: The Wonderful Visit
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: An angel falls into the everyday world of 19th Century England and is bewildered and despirited by human life and society.

books.title: Poor Folk
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: Two poor Russian people exchange letters of love, hope and fear.

books.title: The Portent
books.author: George MacDonald
books.summary: A young Scottish tutor, gifted with a supernatural sense, discovers a haunting connection to a somnambulant noblewoman while residing at a remote English estate.

books.title: My Disillusionment in Russia
books.author: Emma Goldman
books.summary: Emma Goldman recounts her experiences living in the early Soviet Union.

books.title: The Wrong Letter
books.author: Walter S. Masterman
books.summary: A superintendent and an sleuth attempt to solve a locked-room mystery.

books.title: Confessions of an English Opium-Eater
books.author: Thomas De Quincey
books.summary: A portrait of the life of a drug addict in Georgian-era London.

books.title: Two Treatises of Government
books.author: John Locke
books.summary: Locke critiques the concepts of absolute monarchy and divine right, arguing instead for a government founded on natural rights, individual freedom, and the consent of the governed, which protects the fundamental rights of life, liberty, and property.

books.title: Cane
books.author: Jean Toomer
books.summary: An experimental and highly lyrical novel exploring the African-American experience.

books.title: The Works of Max Beerbohm
books.author: Max Beerbohm
books.summary: A collection of satirical essays ridiculing pretentiousness through imitation.

books.title: The Invisible Man
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A scientist named Griffin creates a serum to render himself invisible and subsequently descends into madness.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Akutagawa Ryūnosuke
books.summary: A collection of short fiction by Akutagawa Ryūnosuke, ordered by date of publication.

books.title: The Everlasting Man
books.author: G. K. Chesterton
books.summary: G. K. Chesterton takes us through the history of man, rebuking common secular views and presenting the case for the Catholic Church.

books.title: Recollections of Full Years
books.author: Helen Herron Taft
books.summary: Helen Herron Taft, wife of William Howard Taft, the twenty-seventh President of the United States, recalls her life married to one of America’s most influential political figures.
